what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-source channel, pointing to the image asset at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-03 and noting when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter block with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses an image:// URI scheme
no code symbols or executable content
standouts: References a specific image asset: deck1-2026/p-03 in the design-source channel
Serves as a timestamped provenance stub for an attachment
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured attachment metadata
Names the file with the attachment id (att-82eafb93.md)
Uses a channel-memory/channel_attachments/ path for attachment records
References assets with a custom image:// URI
notes: A lightweight, timestamped provenance stub that registers an image asset under the design-source channel.
questions: What visual content is represented by deck1-2026/p-03?
Who or what process added this attachment?
Does the image:// URI resolve to a stored file, and where?
Are there versioning or replacement rules for this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an attachment registration record that captures channel, asset target, and addition time.
intention_signals: To create a discoverable, timestamped ledger entry that links a channel to a specific image asset for retrieval and audit.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI to locate the asset rather than a conventional filesystem or URL path.
contribution: Provides a stable identifier and channel association for an image asset, supporting retrieval, provenance, and indexing without embedding the image data.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record (att-82eafb93) in the design-source channel that references image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-03 and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and timestamp an image asset so it can be tracked and retrieved within the design-source channel.
